overview: A simple two-component todo application: a Go backend service exposing a REST API for task CRUD and filtering, and a React frontend SPA that consumes it. The frontend communicates with the backend via HTTPS/JSON.
requirements: Users can create tasks with title, optional category, and optional due date
Users can edit and delete tasks
Users can filter tasks by category
Users can filter tasks by due-date timeframe (e.g. today, this week, overdue)
Data persists in-memory for simplicity (single-user demo)

components.name: task-api
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Task API
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
  /tasks:
    get:
      parameters:
        - name: category
          in: query
          schema: { type: string }
        - name: timeframe
          in: query
          schema: { type: string, enum: [today, week, overdue, all] }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Task' }
    post:
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/TaskInput' }
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Task' }
  /tasks/{id}:
    parameters:
      - name: id
        in: path
        required: true
        schema: { type: string }
    get:
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Task' }
        '404': { description: Not found }
    put:
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/TaskInput' }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Updated
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Task' }
    delete:
      responses:
        '204': { description: Deleted }
  /tasks/{id}/complete:
    parameters:
      - name: id
        in: path
        required: true
        schema: { type: string }
    post:
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                completed: { type: boolean }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Task' }
components:
  schemas:
    Task:
      type: object
      properties:
        id: { type: string }
        title: { type: string }
        category: { type: string, nullable: true }
        dueDate: { type: string, format: date, nullable: true }
        completed: { type: boolean }
        createdAt: { type: string, format: date-time }
    TaskInput:
      type: object
      required: [title]
      properties:
        title: { type: string }
        category: { type: string, nullable: true }
        dueDate: { type: string, format: date, nullable: true }
        completed: { type: boolean }
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a Go backend using net/http listening on port 9090. Provide endpoints described in OpenAPI spec for managing tasks. Use an in-memory map[string]Task with a sync.RWMutex for thread safety; generate IDs via crypto/rand or uuid. Endpoints: GET /health returns 200 'ok'; GET /tasks supports query params category (exact match, case-insensitive) and timeframe ('today' = dueDate == today, 'week' = dueDate within next 7 days inclusive of today, 'overdue' = dueDate < today and not completed, 'all' or empty = no filter); POST /tasks validates non-empty title, returns 201 with created task; GET/PUT/DELETE /tasks/{id} with 404 if missing; POST /tasks/{id}/complete toggles or sets completed flag from body. Enable permissive CORS (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *, methods GET,POST,PUT,DELETE,OPTIONS, headers Content-Type) and handle OPTIONS preflight. Return JSON with proper Content-Type. Log requests to stdout. Provide Dockerfile (multi-stage golang:1.22 build, distroless or alpine runtime) exposing 9090.

components.name: task-web
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Task Web
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
components.componentAgentInstructions: Build a React + TypeScript SPA using Vite. Provide a single-page UI to: create tasks (title, optional category, optional dueDate), list all tasks, mark complete via checkbox, edit (inline or modal), delete, and filter by category dropdown and timeframe (All/Today/This Week/Overdue). Use fetch against the backend base URL configured via runtime env (window.__ENV__.API_URL injected by entrypoint script reading TASK_API_URL env var; fall back to '/api'). Show tasks grouped or sorted by due date with completed tasks visually de-emphasized. Use clean modern styling (CSS modules or Tailwind). Include a /health route or static file returning 200 served by nginx. Dockerize with multi-stage: node:20 build → nginx:alpine serve on port 8080; nginx config should serve SPA fallback to index.html and expose /health returning 200.
